national_team.data_quality_flag: Cap/goal totals for England conflict across sources by date of publication - see data_quality_notes. 116 caps / 81 goals is the most recent Wikipedia figure (includes the Croatia match); a separate tracker that includes the following Ghana match shows different appearance/goal context. Treat as approximate pending a single consistent post-Ghana-match source.

stints.notes: Third senior World Cup (2018, 2022, 2026), equalling Billy Wright's record for England World Cup appearances. Scored a brace in the opener vs Croatia (4-2 win), equalling Gary Lineker's England all-time World Cup goal record of 10. Second match was a 0-0 draw vs Ghana in which Kane started but did not score. Group L also includes Panama.

England eliminated 2-1 by France in the quarter-finals; Kane missed a penalty in that match. Scored 2 tournament goals, equalling Wayne Rooney's then all-time England goalscoring record during this World Cup cycle.

Named England captain just before this tournament. Won the Golden Boot (6 goals) as tournament top scorer. Scored a hat-trick vs Panama (England's joint-largest World Cup win). Led England to the semi-finals - their best finish since 1990 at the time.
Topped UEFA Group K. Finished qualifying as England's top scorer with 8 goals. Scored a brace away at Latvia (5-0) on 2025-10-14 to confirm qualification.

career_milestones: England's all-time top goalscorer (81 goals as of his most recent international goal, surpassing Wayne Rooney's 53 in March 2023, in 39 fewer games)
Most goals scored while captaining England (66+)
Most goals scored by England in competitive (non-friendly) matches (71+ of his total)
Most penalty goals for England (24 scored in 28 attempts, as of pre-2026 World Cup figures)
Equalled Gary Lineker's England all-time World Cup goal record (10) during the 2026 group stage
Equalled Billy Wright's record for most England World Cup tournament appearances (3rd World Cup, 2026)
Runner-up at UEFA Euro 2020 and UEFA Euro 2024, both as captain

club_career.notes: Career-best season: 61 goals across all competitions, won the German domestic double (Bundesliga + DFB-Pokal) plus the Super Cup. Scored a hat-trick to seal the DFB-Pokal. Won the European Golden Boot for most goals among Europe's top 5 leagues. Scored a goal every 66 minutes in the Bundesliga this season. Total appearances/starts breakdown not yet confirmed - 36 goals figure is from FotMob's in-season tracker, season still in progress as of 2026-06-23.

Second Bayern season. First major trophy with the club (Bundesliga title). First player in Bundesliga history to finish as top scorer in each of his first two seasons. Full stat breakdown not yet found.

Debut Bayern season after a record Bundesliga transfer fee (100 million euros plus bonuses) from Tottenham in August 2023. Topped the Bundesliga scoring charts (36 goals in 32 appearances) - a record for a newcomer to the league, and won the European Golden Shoe. Starts not separately broken out yet from this prose source.

Final full Tottenham season before the Bayern transfer. Became Tottenham's all-time top scorer this season, passing Jimmy Greaves (267 goals), with the record made undisputed via a brace vs Nottingham Forest (269/270 goals) on 2023-03-11. Finished 2nd in the Premier League scoring charts with 30 goals (behind Haaland's 36), becoming the first player to score 30+ in two separate 38-game PL seasons. Full appearance/starts breakdown not yet found.
Premier League top scorer (Golden Boot) with 23 goals. Full appearance/starts breakdown not yet found.
Tottenham reached the UEFA Champions League final for the first time in club history, lost 0-2 to Liverpool. Stats not yet found - needs manual lookup.
Premier League top scorer (Golden Boot, 2nd of his career). Tottenham finished as Premier League runners-up. Kane named PFA Fans' Player of the Year. Stats not yet found.
First Premier League Golden Boot. Kane's first season as a regular starter under Mauricio Pochettino. Stats not yet found.

club_career.club: Tottenham Hotspur (career total, all seasons combined)
club_career.notes: AGGREGATE PLACEHOLDER, not a single season - represents Kane's entire ~14-year Tottenham career (debut 2009, departed for Bayern August 2023), all competitions combined. 280 goals total = Tottenham's all-time record (passed Jimmy Greaves in Feb 2023). 213 of these came in the Premier League specifically (2nd-highest PL goalscorer all-time, behind Alan Shearer's 260). This entry exists only because most individual Tottenham seasons before 2020-21 are not yet broken out - it should eventually be deleted once enough individual seasons are filled in to avoid double-counting goals.
Loan spell away from Tottenham. Stats not yet found.
Loan spell away from Tottenham. Stats not yet found.
First senior loan spell away from Tottenham, age 17. Stats not yet found - this is Kane's senior debut equivalent to Haaland's Bryne FK or Mbappe's Monaco debut season.

data_quality_notes: ENGLAND CAP/GOAL TOTALS CONFLICT ACROSS SOURCES: Wikipedia (most recent, includes Croatia match) says 116 caps / 81 goals. A World Cup-specific tracker dated pre-Ghana-match says 112 caps / 78 goals 'as of 16 November 2025'. Another source citing 'the 12 months before the World Cup' says 79 goals. These do not all refer to the same moment in time and have not been reconciled into one number - caps_total/goals_total above use the most recent Wikipedia figure as the best available, but should be re-verified once a single source post-dates the Ghana match (2026-06-23, 0-0 draw, Kane did not score).

MAJOR STRUCTURAL GAP: most of Kane's ~14-year Tottenham career (2009-2023) is not broken into individual seasons with full stats. Several known-individual seasons (2015-16, 2016-17, 2018-19, 2020-21, 2022-23) are listed with whatever single facts (usually just a goals total) could be found, all flagged needs_breakdown. An additional AGGREGATE PLACEHOLDER entry covers his entire Tottenham career total (280 goals, all competitions) - this is NOT a normal season-stint and will double-count if all individual seasons are later filled in; it should be deleted once that happens, kept only as a stopgap so the career total is visible to a user before full backfill.

Loan spells (Leyton Orient, Millwall, Leicester City, Norwich City) are sparsely documented across all sources checked - Norwich City couldn't be matched to a specific season at all and is omitted pending further research; Leyton Orient/Millwall/Leicester are included as needs_breakdown placeholders by club name only.

Bayern Munich 2025-26 (current season): 36 goals/5 assists/2382 minutes from FotMob's in-season tracker, consistent with Bundesliga.com's reporting of the same season. Appearances/starts not yet confirmed.
Bayern Munich 2023-24: appearances (32) and goals (36) are both confirmed and consistent across two independent sources (FC Bayern official site and Olympics.com); starts not separately given.

No structured per-season stat tables (the FotMob-style format used for Haaland's Man City/Dortmund/Salzburg seasons) have been supplied for Kane yet - if available, especially for Bayern's 3 seasons or Tottenham's most statistically rich seasons (2022-23, 2020-21, 2016-17), these would resolve several of the flagged gaps at once.
Per established schema: appearances and starts stored independently where known; advanced stat tier reserved for seasons with a detailed feed (none populated yet for Kane).

Sources: Wikipedia (player page + international goals list), FC Bayern official site, Bundesliga.com, FotMob, FBref, Britannica, England Football (englandfootball.com), Olympics.com, StatMuse, Metro League, Striker Report, MatchBingo, TheSoccerWorldCups.com (June 2026).
